name: wf-ship-it
title: Ship Workflow
description: Workflow skill that runs repository checks, release hygiene, release readiness, and then the release-only commit/tag/publish flow as one default entrypoint. Use when the user wants release preparation and publication without manually chaining review and publish skills.
purpose: Compose repository prechecks, release hygiene, release judgement, and release publication into one default release workflow.
when_to_use: Need one default release entrypoint instead of manually chaining `wf-ship-ready-check` and `release-publish`.
Need repo checks, hygiene gates, release-only commit policy, tag creation, and publish status handled as one explicit flow.
Need `TARGET_BRANCHES` to be the single branch source of truth for both review and publish phases.
do_not_use: Need only release judgement without any branch or tag mutation; use `wf-ship-ready-check` instead.
Need only low-level publish execution after some other gate system already ran; use `release-publish` instead.
Cannot tolerate branch, tag, or remote mutation in the current run.

required_inputs.name: TARGET_BRANCHES
required_inputs.description: Branches involved in the release, such as source/dev and target/main.

required_inputs.name: RELEASE_TAG
required_inputs.description: Explicit tag to publish, such as `v1.0.0`.

required_inputs.name: RELEASE_SCOPE
required_inputs.description: Release slice to judge. Defaults to `repo`.

required_inputs.name: HYGIENE_SCOPE
required_inputs.description: Scope for release hygiene checks. Defaults to `repo`.

required_inputs.name: REQUIRED_DOCS
required_inputs.description: Release-blocking docs that must be upgraded.

required_inputs.name: LEGACY_PATTERNS
required_inputs.description: Legacy names or files that must not survive the release.

required_inputs.name: SURFACE_CONTRACTS
required_inputs.description: Public-surface contracts such as registry/docs parity or root README ownership.

required_inputs.name: REQUIRED_CHECKS
required_inputs.description: Checks that must pass before the release branch and tag are mutated.

required_inputs.name: PUBLISH_TARGET
required_inputs.description: Whether to stop at a local tag, push a tag only, or publish a GitHub release.

required_inputs.name: REMOTE_NAME
required_inputs.description: Remote expected to receive release refs. Default is origin.

required_inputs.name: RELEASE_NOTES_SOURCE
required_inputs.description: How release notes should be sourced.

required_inputs.name: RELEASE_NOTES_PATH
required_inputs.description: Path to release notes when using path mode.

input_contract_notes: `TARGET_BRANCHES` must include one `{ROLE: source}` and one `{ROLE: target}`. The workflow derives `SOURCE_BRANCH` and `TARGET_BRANCH` for the publish phase from that one input.
`RELEASE_SCOPE` and `HYGIENE_SCOPE` default to `repo` when omitted because this workflow is meant to be the simplest end-to-end release entrypoint.
Use this workflow when the public branch policy matters. It keeps `main` release-only by routing publication through `release-publish`.
If only the candidate tag is fixed and the rest should stay standard, prefer `RELEASE_BUMP=explicit-tag` with `RELEASE_TAG` rather than bypassing the workflow.

structured_outputs.name: CHECK_REPORTS
structured_outputs.description: Underlying repo, hygiene, readiness, and publish stage reports.

structured_outputs.name: CLEANUP_REPORT
structured_outputs.description: Release-hygiene actions such as legacy removal, stale-doc deletion, or registry synchronization.

structured_outputs.name: RELEASE_COMMITS
structured_outputs.description: Recorded commits for the source branch and the release-only target branch commit.

structured_outputs.name: PUBLISH_RESULTS
structured_outputs.description: Tag, push, and GitHub release outcomes.

structured_outputs.name: RELEASE_STATUS
structured_outputs.description: Overall execution state after checks, branch sync, tag, and publish attempts.

structured_outputs.name: EXPANDED_EXECUTION_PATH
structured_outputs.description: Actual execution path used by the workflow.

output_contract_notes: `CHECK_REPORTS` should preserve the repo, hygiene, readiness, and publish stage identities instead of collapsing them into a single paragraph.
`RELEASE_DECISION` must stay traceable to the review stages even when publication later fails.
`RELEASE_STATUS` must report prepared or blocked honestly when review passes but publish cannot finish.
`EXPANDED_EXECUTION_PATH` must preserve actual workflow order and include `release-publish` at the end.
neutrality_rules: Preserve the neutrality rules of the underlying repository, hygiene, readiness, and publication skills.
Do not bypass a blocked review gate just because publication inputs are present.
Do not report a release as published unless branch push, tag push, and release publication were actually confirmed.
execution_constraints: Derive publish branch inputs from `TARGET_BRANCHES`; do not require the user to repeat them in the same request.
Keep the target branch release-only by delegating publication to `release-publish`.
If repo, hygiene, or readiness gates block the release, stop before mutating branches, tags, remotes, or release hosts.
If docs or public-surface cleanup is part of the release contract, keep publication blocked until those checks pass.
default_program: [stages: preflight>detect>analyze>plan>review>handoff>audit | scope: repo | policy: evidence,safety-gates,quality-gates{docs,release,tests,security},approval-gates{explicit,no-fallback},deterministic-output | lens: release-gatekeeper | output: md(contract=v1)]
mandatory_rules: Use `TARGET_BRANCHES` as the single branch source of truth for both review and publish stages.
Run required checks before mutating the target branch or creating the final public tag.
Record exact branch, tag, and publish refs in the final report.
